py7zr is a library and utility to support 7zip archive compression, decompression,
encryption and decryption written by Python programming language.

## Discussion Forum

You are welcome to join discussions on project forum/builtin-board at
<https://github.com/miurahr/py7zr/discussions>

You can see announcements of new releases, questions and answers, and
new feature ideas. When you doubt for usage of py7zr library with unclear
manuals, please feel easy to raise question on forum.

## Compression algorithms

`py7zr` supports algorithms and filters which [lzma module] and [liblzma] support,
and supports BZip2 and Deflate that are implemented in python core libraries,
It also supports ZStandard, Brotli and PPMd with third party libraries.

`py7zr` is also able to encrypt and decrypt data using 3rd party encryption library.

### Supported algorithms

- compress
  : - LZMA2
    - LZMA
    - Bzip2
    - Deflate
    - Copy
    - ZStandard
    - Brotli
    - PPMd
    - Enhanced Deflate (Experimental)
- crypt
  : - 7zAES
- Filters
  : - Delta
    - BCJ(X86,ARMT,ARM,PPC,SPARC,IA64)

:::{note}
- A feature handling symbolic link is basically compatible with `p7zip` implementation,
  but not work with original 7-zip because the original does not implement the feature.
- `py7zr` try checking symbolic links strictly and raise ValueError when bad link is requested,
  but it does **not** guarantee to block all the bad cases.
- ZStandard and Brotli is not default methods of 7-zip, so these archives are considered
  not to be compatible with original 7-zip on windows/p7zip on linux/mac.
- Enhanced Deflate is also known as `DEFLATE64` {sup}`TM` that is a registered trademark of `PKWARE, Inc.`
- Enhanced Deflate is tested only on CPython. It is disabled on PyPy.
:::

### Not supported algorithms

- BCJ2 (Standard [lzma module] does not provide).

## Install

You can install py7zr as usual other libraries using pip.

```shell
$ pip install py7zr
```

OR, alternatively using conda:

```shell
$ conda install -c conda-forge py7zr
```

## CLI Usage

You can run command script py7zr like as follows;

- List archive contents

```shell
$ py7zr l test.7z
```

- Extract archive

```shell
$ py7zr x test.7z
```

- Extract archive with password

```shell
$ py7zr x -P test.7z
  password?: ****
```

- Create and compress to archive

```shell
$ py7zr c target.7z test_dir
```

- Create multi-volume archive

```shell
$ py7zr c -v 500k target.7z test_dir
```

- Test archive

```shell
$ py7zr t test.7z
```

- Append files to archive

```shell
$ py7zr a test.7z test_dir
```

- Show information

```shell
$ py7zr i
```

- Show version

```shell
$ py7zr --version
```

## SevenZipFile Class Usage

py7zr is a library which can use in your python application.

### Decompression/Decryption

Here is a code snippet how to decompress some file in your application.

```python
import py7zr

archive = py7zr.SevenZipFile('sample.7z', mode='r')
archive.extractall(path="/tmp")
archive.close()
```

You can also use 'with' block because py7zr provide context manager(v0.6 and later).

```python
import py7zr

with py7zr.SevenZipFile('sample.7z', mode='r') as z:
    z.extractall()

with py7zr.SevenZipFile('target.7z', 'w') as z:
    z.writeall('./base_dir')
```

`py7zr` also supports extraction of single or selected files by 'extract(targets=\['file path'\])'.
Note: if you specify only a file but not a parent directory, it will fail.

```python
import py7zr
import re

filter_pattern = re.compile(r'<your/target/file_and_directories/regex/expression>')
with py7zr.SevenZipFile('archive.7z', 'r') as archive:
    allfiles = archive.getnames()
    selective_files = [f for f in allfiles if filter_pattern.match(f)]
    archive.extract(targets=selective_files)
```

py7zr support an extraction of password protected archive.(v0.6 and later)

```python
import py7zr

with py7zr.SevenZipFile('encrypted.7z', mode='r', password='secret') as z:
    z.extractall()
```

### Compression/Encryption

Here is a code snippet how to produce archive.

```python
import py7zr

with py7zr.SevenZipFile('target.7z', 'w') as archive:
    archive.writeall('/path/to/base_dir', 'base')
```

To create encrypted archive, please pass a password.

```python
import py7zr

with py7zr.SevenZipFile('target.7z', 'w', password='secret') as archive:
    archive.writeall('/path/to/base_dir', 'base')
```

To create archive with algorithms such as zstandard, you can call with custom filter.

```python
import py7zr

my_filters = [{"id": py7zr.FILTER_ZSTD}]
another_filters = [{"id": py7zr.FILTER_ARM}, {"id": py7zr.FILTER_LZMA2, "preset": 7}]
with py7zr.SevenZipFile('target.7z', 'w', filters=my_filters) as archive:
    archive.writeall('/path/to/base_dir', 'base')
```

## shutil helper

py7zr also support `shutil`  interface.

```python
from py7zr import pack_7zarchive, unpack_7zarchive
import shutil

# register file format at first.
shutil.register_archive_format('7zip', pack_7zarchive, description='7zip archive')
shutil.register_unpack_format('7zip', ['.7z'], unpack_7zarchive)

# extraction
shutil.unpack_archive('test.7z', '/tmp')

# compression
shutil.make_archive('target', '7zip', 'src')
```

## Requirements

`py7zr` uses a python3 standard [lzma module] for extraction and compression.
The standard lzma module uses [liblzma] that support core compression algorithm of 7zip.

Minimum required version is Python 3.8.

`py7zr` tested on Linux, macOS, Windows and Ubuntu aarch64.

It hopefully works on M1 Mac too.

Recommended versions are:

- CPython 3.8.0 and later.
- PyPy3.8-7.3.8 and later.

Following fixes are included in these versions, and it is not fixed on python3.6.

- [BPO-21872]: LZMA library sometimes fails to decompress a file
- [PyPy3-3090]: lzma.LZMADecomporessor.decompress does not respect max_length
- [PyPy3-3242]: '\_lzma_cffi' has no function named 'lzma_stream_encoder'

Following improvements are included in CPython 3.10

- [BPO-41486]: Faster bz2/lzma/zlib via new output buffering

## Dependencies

There are several dependencies to support algorithms and CLI expressions.

| Package           | Purpose                         |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------- |
| [PyCryptodomex]   | 7zAES encryption                |
| [PyZstd]          | ZStandard compression           |
| [PyPPMd]          | PPMd compression                |
| [Brotli]          | Brotli compression (CPython)    |
| [BrotliCFFI]      | Brotli compression (PyPy)       |
| [inflate64]       | Enhanced deflate compression    |
| [pybcj]           | BCJ filters                     |
| [multivolumefile] | Multi-volume archive read/write |
| [texttable]       | CLI formatter                   |

## Performance

You can find a compression and decompression benchmark results at
\[Github issue\](<https://github.com/miurahr/py7zr/issues/297>) and \[wiki page\](<https://github.com/miurahr/py7zr/wiki/Benchmarks>)

py7zr works well, but slower than `7-zip` and `p7zip` C/C++ implementation by several reasons.
When compression/decompression **speed** is important, it is recommended to use these
alternatives through `subprocess.run` python interface.

py7zr consumes some memory to decompress and compress data. It requires about 300MiB - 700MiB free memory to work well at least.

## Use Cases

- [aqtinstall] Another (unofficial) Qt (aqt) CLI Installer on multi-platforms.
- [PreNLP] Preprocessing Library for Natural Language Processing
- [mlox]  a tool for sorting and analyzing Morrowind plugin load order

## Security

Please find a [Security Policy] of this project.

Version 0.20.0, 0.19.0, 0.18.10 or before has a [vulnerability for path traversal]  attack.
Details are on "CVE-2022-44900: path traversal vulnerability in py7zr" [disclose article] .

Affected versions  are vulnerable to Directory Traversal due to insufficient checks in the 'py7zr.py' and 'helpers.py' files

You are recommend to update immediately to version 0.20.2 or later

I really appreciate Mr. Matteo Cosentino for notification and corporation on security improvement.

functions.name: py7zr.properties.is_64bit
documentation: check if running platform is 64bit python.

functions.name: py7zr.properties.is_pypy369later
documentation: check if running platform is PyPY and python 3.6.9 and later

functions.name: py7zr.properties.get_default_blocksize
documentation:
Return a safe buffer size for pass to decompress/compress modules.
We check several conditions;

1. 32bit python

> It sometimes fails with memory_error
> when decompress/compress large file (hundreds of MiB) on 32bit python.
> We reduce buffer size smaller to avoid memory_error.
> @see issue <https://github.com/miurahr/py7zr/issues/370>

2. CPython

> CPython implementation of 3.7.5 fixed a lzma module bug
> that is not respect max_length parameter.
> When buffer size is larger than default of the module,
> max_length can be a value to lead the bug, so we set it
> lzma module default = 32768 bytes.
> @see BPO-21872: LZMA library sometimes fails to decompress a file
>
> > <https://bugs.python.org/issue21872>
>
> @see <https://github.com/miurahr/py7zr/issues/272>

3. PyPy

> PyPy 7.2 (python 3.6.9) fixed a lzma module's bug as same as
> CPython above.
> We set buffer size is as default size of the module to avoid the bugs.
> @see PyPy3-3090: lzma.LZMADecomporessor.decompress does not respect max_length
>
> > <https://foss.heptapod.net/pypy/pypy/-/issues/3090>
>
> @see <https://github.com/miurahr/py7zr/pull/114>

```
return

recommended buffer size as int.
```

\:return: allowed chunk size in bytes.

documentation: report a start of preparation event such as making list of files and looking into its properties.

documentation: report a start event of specified archive file and its input bytes.

methods.name: py7zr.callbacks.Callback.report_update
documentation:
report an event when large file is being extracted more than 1 second or when extraction is finished.
receives a number of decompressed bytes since the last update.

documentation: report an end event of specified archive file and its output bytes.

documentation: report an warning event with its message

documentation: Base class for exceptions.

classes.name: py7zr.exceptions.CrcError
documentation:
Exception raised for CRC error when decompression.

Attributes:
  expected -- expected CRC bytes
  actual -- actual CRC data
  filename -- filename that has CRC error

classes.name: py7zr.exceptions.UnsupportedCompressionMethodError
documentation:
Exception raised for unsupported compression parameter given.

Attributes:
  data -- unknown property data
  message -- explanation of error

documentation: Calculate CRC32 of strings with arbitrary lengths.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers._calculate_key1
documentation: Calculate 7zip AES encryption key. Base implementation.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers._calculate_key2
documentation:
Calculate 7zip AES encryption key.
It uses ctypes and memoryview buffer and zero-copy technology on Python.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers._calculate_key3
documentation:
Calculate 7zip AES encryption key.
Concat values in order to reduce number of calls of Hash.update().

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.filetime_to_dt
documentation: Convert Windows NTFS file time into python datetime object.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.islink
documentation:
Cross-platform islink implementation.
Support Windows NT symbolic links and reparse points.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.readlink
documentation:
Cross-platform compat implementation of os.readlink and Path.readlink().
Support Windows NT symbolic links and reparse points.
When called with path argument as pathlike(str), return result as a pathlike(str).
When called with Path object, return also Path object.
When called with path argument as bytes, return result as a bytes.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.remove_relative_path_marker
documentation: Removes './' from the beginning of a path-like string

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.canonical_path
documentation: Return a canonical path of target argument.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.is_relative_to
documentation: Return True when path is relative to other path, otherwise False.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.get_sanitized_output_path
documentation:
check f.filename has invalid directory traversals
When condition is not satisfied, raise Bad7zFile

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.check_archive_path
documentation:
Check arcname argument is valid for archive.
It should not be absolute, if so it returns False.
It should not be evil traversal attack path.
Otherwise, returns True.

functions.name: py7zr.helpers.is_path_valid
documentation:
Check if target path is valid against parent path.
It returns False when target path has '..' and point out of parent path.
Otherwise, returns True.

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.read_real_uint64
documentation: read 8 bytes, return unpacked value as a little endian unsigned long long, and raw data.

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.read_uint32
documentation: read 4 bytes, return unpacked value as a little endian unsigned long, and raw data.

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.write_uint32
documentation: write uint32 value in 4 bytes.

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.read_uint64
documentation: read UINT64, definition show in write_uint64()

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.write_real_uint64
documentation: write 8 bytes, as an unsigned long long.

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.write_uint64
documentation:
UINT64 means real UINT64 encoded with the following scheme:

|  Size of encoding sequence depends from first byte:
|  First_Byte  Extra_Bytes        Value
|  (binary)
|  0xxxxxxx               : ( xxxxxxx           )
|  10xxxxxx    BYTE y[1]  : (  xxxxxx << (8 * 1)) + y
|  110xxxxx    BYTE y[2]  : (   xxxxx << (8 * 2)) + y
|  ...
|  1111110x    BYTE y[6]  : (       x << (8 * 6)) + y
|  11111110    BYTE y[7]  :                         y
|  11111111    BYTE y[8]  :                         y

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.read_utf16
documentation: read a utf-16 string from file

functions.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.write_utf16
documentation: write a utf-16 string to file

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.WriteWithCrc
documentation: Thin wrapper for file object to calculate crc32 when write called.

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.PackInfo
documentation: information about packed streams

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.Bond
documentation:
Represent bindings between two methods.
bonds[i] = (incoder, outstream)
means
methods[i].stream[outstream] output data go to method[incoder].stream[0]

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.Folder
documentation:
a "Folder" represents a stream of compressed data.
coders: list of coder
num_coders: length of coders
coder: hash list
keys of coders:  method, numinstreams, numoutstreams, properties
unpacksizes: uncompressed sizes of outstreams

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.UnpackInfo
documentation: combines multiple folders

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.SubstreamsInfo
documentation: defines the substreams of a folder

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.StreamsInfo
documentation: information about compressed streams

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.HeaderStreamsInfo
documentation: Header version of StreamsInfo

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.FilesInfo

methods.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.Header._read
documentation:
Decode header data or encoded header data from buffer.
When buffer consist of encoded buffer, it get stream data
from it and call itself recursively
the archive header

classes.name: py7zr.archiveinfo.SignatureHeader
documentation: The SignatureHeader class hold information of a signature header of archive.

classes.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.file_properties
documentation:
Return file properties as a hash object. Following keys are included: ‘readonly’, ‘is_directory’,
‘posix_mode’, ‘archivable’, ‘emptystream’, ‘filename’, ‘creationtime’, ‘lastaccesstime’,
‘lastwritetime’, ‘attributes’

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.filename
documentation: return filename of archive file.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.emptystream
documentation: True if file is empty(0-byte file), otherwise False

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.compressed
documentation: Compressed size

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.crc32
documentation: CRC of archived file(optional)

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.archivable
documentation: File has a Windows `archive` flag.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.is_directory
documentation: True if file is a directory, otherwise False.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.readonly
documentation: True if file is readonly, otherwise False.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.has_strdata
documentation: True if file content is set by writestr() method otherwise False.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.is_symlink
documentation: True if file is a symbolic link, otherwise False.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.is_junction
documentation: True if file is a junction/reparse point on windows, otherwise False.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.is_socket
documentation: True if file is a socket, otherwise False.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.lastwritetime
documentation: Return last written timestamp of a file.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.posix_mode
documentation:
posix mode when a member has a unix extension property, or None
\:return: Return file stat mode can be set by os.chmod()

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFile.st_fmt
documentation:
---
return: Return the portion of the file mode that describes the file type
---

Represent each files metadata inside archive file.
It holds file properties; filename, permissions, and type whether
it is directory, link or normal file.

Instances of the {class}`ArchiveFile` class are returned by iterating {attr}`files_list` of
{class}`SevenZipFile` objects.
Each object stores information about a single member of the 7z archive. Most of users use {meth}`extractall()`.

The class also hold an archive parameter where file is exist in
archive file folder(container).

classes.name: py7zr.py7zr.ArchiveFileList
documentation: Iteratable container of ArchiveFile.

documentation: Hold archive information
Exported Classes

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile._write_header
documentation: Write header and update signature header.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.getnames
documentation:
Return the members of the archive as a list of their names. It has
the same order as the list returned by getmembers().
The public methods which SevenZipFile provides:

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.namelist
documentation: Return a list of archive members by name.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.list
documentation: Returns contents information

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.extractall
documentation:
Extract all members from the archive to the current working
directory and set owner, modification time and permissions on
directories afterwards. `path` specifies a different directory
to extract to.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.writeall
documentation: Write files in target path into archive.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.write
documentation: Write single target file into archive.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.close
documentation:
Flush all the data into archive and close it.
When close py7zr start reading target and writing actual archive file.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.SevenZipFile.reset
documentation: When read mode, it reset file pointer, decompress worker and decompressor
The SevenZipFile Class provides an interface to 7z archives.

classes.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker.extract
documentation: Extract worker method to handle 7zip folder and decompress each files.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker.extract_single
documentation: Single thread extractor that takes file lists in single 7zip folder.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker._extract_single
documentation:
Single thread extractor that takes file lists in single 7zip folder.
this may raise exception.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker._check
documentation: delayed execution of crc check.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker.decompress
documentation:
decompressor wrapper called from extract method.

:parameter fp: archive source file pointer
:parameter folder: Folder object that have decompressor object.
:parameter fq: output file pathlib.Path
:parameter size: uncompressed size of target file.
:parameter compressed_size: compressed size of target file.
:parameter src_end: end position of the folder
:parameter q: the queue for the reporter

:returns None

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker._find_link_target
documentation: Find the target member of a symlink or hardlink member in the archive.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker.archive
documentation: Run archive task for specified 7zip folder.

methods.name: py7zr.py7zr.Worker.register_filelike
